claim: Researchers at Caldera University found that daily 30-minute walking reduced systolic blood pressure by an average of 12 mmHg in adults with hypertension over 12 weeks.

corpus.title: Update 2: Caldera University — Spread Chain
corpus.snippet: Researchers at Caldera University found that daily 30-minute walking reduced sys...
corpus.content:
Caldera University ANNOUNCES UPDATE: SCIENTIFIC DEVELOPMENTS

Researchers at Caldera University found that daily 30-minute walking reduced systolic blood pressure by an average of 12 mmHg in adults with hypertension over 12 weeks. The announcement comes as the developing scientific story. Observers say this could have significant implications for scientific stakeholders. The full impact is expected to become clear over the coming months.

corpus.title: Walking Intervention and Blood Pressure: A Randomised Controlled Trial
corpus.snippet: Researchers at Caldera University found that daily 30-minute walking reduced systolic blood pressure by an average of 6 ...
corpus.content:
RANDOMISED CONTROLLED TRIAL — American Heart Journal
Published: January 22, 2024

STUDY DESIGN: 12-week RCT; n=210 adults with Stage 1 hypertension (SBP 130-159 mmHg). Intervention: 30 min brisk walking daily. Control: usual care.

PRIMARY OUTCOME: Change in systolic blood pressure (SBP) at 12 weeks.
Results: Intervention group mean SBP reduction: -6.2 mmHg (SD ±3.1)
Control group mean SBP change: -1.1 mmHg (SD ±2.8)
Between-group difference: -5.1 mmHg (p<0.001)

Caldera University, Department of Preventive Medicine.

corpus.title: Secondary Official Record — Caldera University
corpus.snippet: Secondary analysis of Caldera University data with minor discrepancy.
corpus.content:
OFFICIAL RECORD — Caldera University
Date: 2024-01-22

This record confirms Researchers at Caldera University found that daily 30-minute walking reduced systolic blood pressure by an average of 6 mmHg in adults with hypertension over 12 weeks. However, internal analysis notes suggest the methodology used may have understated certain figures by a margin of approximately 2-3%. Further review is pending.

Researchers at Caldera University found that daily 30-minute walking reduced systolic blood pressure by an average of 6 mmHg in adults with hypertension over 12 weeks.
